> Controlled Unclassified Information, Pre-Decisional, Internal VA Use Only - Working DRAFT

# Interface Control Document — FMS Obligation/Disbursement Extract -> iFAMS (Momentum) Obligation Import

*Document date: 2026-06-11 — generated by the Integration & Conversion Factory from the slice's live code, contracts, and reconciliation artifacts; structure per the customer ICD automation spec (`factory/reference/customer-artifacts/icd_schema.json`).*

## Version Tracker
| sequence_number | status | author | date | change_summary |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1.0 | Draft | Integration & Conversion Factory (generated) | 2026-06-11 | Initial generated draft from the obligation/disbursement conversion slice. |

## Executive Summary
**Business context.** VA FMBT is migrating core financial management from the legacy FMS to iFAMS (CGI Momentum). The obligation/disbursement spending chain is the highest-stakes data family in that migration: disbursing beyond obligated authority is an Antideficiency-Act-class finding.

**Integration objective.** Convert the daily FMS obligation/disbursement extract into the Momentum obligation-import contract with provable correctness: row accounting, dollar control totals to the cent, per-obligation funding gates, and a typed reject ledger with no silent drops.

**Stakeholders.** VA OFM/Finance (business owner), FMBT ID&C team (interface delivery), CGI SI team (Momentum target), Guidehouse + Cognition (integration & conversion factory), interface partner system owners.

## 1. Introduction
**1.2 Purpose.** This ICD defines the data exchange between the legacy FMS obligation/disbursement extract and the iFAMS (Momentum) obligation import, including mapping, controls, reconciliation, and error handling.

**1.3 Scope — in scope:**
- Daily FMS obligation and disbursement extract file (fixed-width, OBL-DISBURSEMENT-REC layout)
- Transformation to the Momentum obligation-import pipe-delimited contract
- Reconciliation evidence: row accounting, $ control totals, per-obligation funding gate
- Typed reject ledger and resubmission flow

**Out of scope:**
- Application logic modernization (CACI lane)
- Momentum platform configuration (CGI lane)
- Non-financial interfaces of the partner system

**1.4 Assumptions:**
- Interface is outbound-only from FMS to iFAMS (one direction, file-based).
- SFTP transport via the VA ESB is available in all environments.
- The Momentum obligation-import contract version is stable within a wave.
- Synthetic reference data (USSGL whitelist, fund crosswalk) will be replaced by authoritative fiscal-year charts before production cutover (open questions Q-REF-1, Q-REF-2).

**1.5 Constraints:**
- Batch window: extract must complete load simulation before the Momentum nightly import cutoff.
- All money handled as Decimal; control totals compared to the cent.
- No production data in this repository; all fixtures are synthetic.

| Authoritative Momentum import contract (ICD) not yet supplied by CGI. | Tracked as open questions Q-MOM-1/Q-MOM-2; generated contract used as stand-in. |

## 2. System Identification
**2.1 VA system — FMS (Financial Management System) — obligation/disbursement subsystem.** Legacy VA core financial system recording obligations and disbursements against appropriated funds; produces a daily fixed-width extract of spending-chain activity. Mainframe COBOL/Pro*COBOL batch processing with fixed-width record layouts (OBL-DISBURSEMENT-REC copybook) and Oracle persistence.

**2.2 iFAMS.** iFAMS (CGI Momentum SaaS on Azure) is the FM QSMO-approved target platform; it ingests obligation documents via its batch import contracts and enforces funds control. ESB functions:
- Proxy submission of extract files from the partner boundary
- Transport logging and delivery acknowledgement
- SFTP endpoint hosting and routing to the Momentum import landing zone

## 3. Interface Definition
**3.1 Legacy integration.** FMS nightly batch produces the obligation/disbursement extract; legacy jobs validate and post to FMS ledgers. Downstream consumers receive flat-file copies.

**3.2 Future integration.** The factory converts the same extract through parse -> map/validate -> reconcile -> emit -> simulated load. Accepted lines are emitted in the Momentum obligation-import wire format; rejects land in a typed ledger for source-system correction and resubmission.

**3.3.1 Operations:**
| id | name | purpose | trigger | direction | interfaces_impacted |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| OP-1 | Daily obligation/disbursement load | Convert and load the daily FMS extract into Momentum. | time | outbound | Momentum obligation import |
| OP-2 | Reject resubmission | Reprocess corrected records after source-system fix. | event | outbound | Momentum obligation import |

**3.3.2 Data transfer:**
| protocol | routing | packaging |
| --- | --- | --- |
| SFTP | FMS batch -> VA ESB -> Momentum import landing zone | pipe-delimited |

**3.3.3 Transaction types:**
| name | usage | frequency | volume_estimate |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| OBLIGATION | Establishes/increases obligated authority for a document line. | Daily batch | TBD-CUSTOMER (no volume data in customer inventory) |
| DISBURSEMENT | Records outlays against an existing obligation. | Daily batch | TBD-CUSTOMER (no volume data in customer inventory) |

**3.3.4 Data exchanges:**
| producer | consumer | format | schedule | acknowledgement |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| FMS | iFAMS (Momentum obligation import) | pipe-delimited per MOMENTUM-OBLIGATION-IMPORT contract | Daily, post-FMS-nightly-batch | yes |

**3.3.5 Data compliance.** All emitted values conform to iFAMS reference data: USSGL accounts validated against the fiscal-year chart, legacy funds mapped through the authoritative crosswalk, dates normalized to ISO-8601. Non-conforming records are rejected with typed reasons, never transformed by guess.

**3.3.6 Precedence:**
- Obligation lines for a document load before disbursement lines (funding gate dependency).
- Rejected lines never block accepted lines in the same batch.

**Criticality:**
- High — spending-chain integrity; failed loads can stop disbursement processing and create ADA exposure.

**3.3.7 Communication methods:**
- SFTP (batch file)
- Email notification of batch disposition (ESB)

**3.3.8 Security & integrity:**
| id | requirement | implementation_detail | transmission_medium | data_protection | auditability |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| SEC-1 | Encrypt all interface traffic in transit. | SFTP (SSH2) between partner boundary, ESB, and Momentum landing zone. | VA network / ESB SFTP | TLS/SSH encryption in transit; CUI handling at rest | ESB transfer logs retained per VA records schedule |
| SEC-2 | No credentials in code or files. | Managed secrets for SFTP/service accounts; parameterized DB access. | n/a | Secrets manager, least privilege | Access logging on secret retrieval |

**3.3.9 Logging & audit:**
| platform | logging_detail | audit_detail | poc | monitoring_instructions |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| SYSTEM | FMS batch job logs: extract row counts and $ totals. | Batch control report retained with the extract. | TBD-SME | Compare extract control totals to factory recon report. |
| ESB | Transfer logs: file name, size, checksum, timestamps. | Delivery acknowledgement records. | TBD-SME | Alert on missing daily file by cutoff time. |
| iFAMS | Momentum import job log: accepted/rejected counts. | Import batch audit trail keyed by batch id. | TBD-SME | Reconcile Momentum-loaded totals to factory emit totals. |

## 4. Operational Considerations
**4.1 Data volumes:**
| service_name | transaction_volume | line_counts | hours_of_operation | frequency |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Daily obligation/disbursement load | TBD-CUSTOMER (gap: customer inventory has no volumes) | TBD-CUSTOMER | Nightly batch window (ET) | Daily |

**Peak periods:**
- Fiscal year-end (September): expect multiples of typical daily volume (quantification pending customer data).
- Month-end close: elevated correction/resubmission traffic.

**4.2 Data contention.** Same-document collisions are possible when an obligation modification and a disbursement for one document arrive in the same batch. Mitigations:
- Deterministic intra-batch ordering: obligations before disbursements per document.
- Idempotent restart: re-running a batch cannot double-post (natural-key de-duplication).

**4.3 Performance requirements:**
| id | requirement_text | metric | threshold | measurement_method |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| PERF-1 | Daily extract converted, reconciled, and load-simulated within the batch window. | End-to-end conversion wall-clock time | Within nightly window ahead of Momentum import cutoff | Factory run telemetry per batch |

**4.4 Priorities:** Spending-chain integrity over throughput; Reject visibility over auto-correction

**Dependencies:** FMS nightly batch completion; ESB SFTP availability; Momentum import window

**4.5 Service availability:**
| system | availability | maintenance_window | scheduled_outage_process |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| iFAMS | Per CGI Momentum SaaS SLA | TBD-CUSTOMER | Hold-and-queue at ESB |
| ESB | 24x7 with maintenance windows | TBD-CUSTOMER | Retransmit after window |
| FMS | Nightly batch | TBD-CUSTOMER | Next-day catch-up batch |

**4.6 Scheduling & operations (runbook):**
| job_name | schedule | preconditions | file_locations | expected_counts | escalation_steps |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| FCT-OBL-DAILY | TBD-SME (align to FMS nightly batch completion) | FMS extract present and checksum-verified | ESB landing zone (path per environment connect procedure) | Must equal FMS batch control report counts | TBD-SME on-call rotation |

**4.7 Upgrades / future waves:**
| area | impact | action_required |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Momentum import contract version | Field additions/renames change the emit contract. | Regenerate mapping + this ICD from the updated contract; rerun golden-file regression. |

## 5. Error Handling & Reconciliation
**5.1 Error handling:**
| service_name | platform | detection_method | common_error_codes | correction_process | resubmission_method | responsible_team |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Daily obligation/disbursement load | SYSTEM | Factory mapper validation (typed reject reasons) | BAD_DATE, BAD_FUND, BAD_PERIOD, BAD_POP, BAD_TXN_TYPE, BAD_USSGL, MISSING_OBLIGATION_NO, MISSING_VENDOR, NON_NUMERIC, ZERO_AMOUNT | Correct in FMS source data; never hand-edit the extract. | Corrected records included in next daily extract or ad-hoc resubmission batch | Interface Partner + Finance |
| Daily obligation/disbursement load | ESB | Missing-file and checksum alerts | TRANSFER_TIMEOUT, CHECKSUM_MISMATCH | Retransmit from source | ESB-managed retry | ESB operations |
| Daily obligation/disbursement load | iFAMS | Momentum import job status + post-load reconciliation | IMPORT_REJECT, FUNDS_CONTROL_FAIL | Triage against factory recon report; correct at source | Resubmit corrected batch | ID&C + CGI SI |

**5.2 Reconciliation:**
| scope | control_totals_used | reports | frequency | owner |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| file-level | lines_in == loaded + rejected; obligation $ and disbursement $ totals to the cent (legacy vs emitted) | Factory reconciliation report (JSON) per batch | Every batch (daily) | Integration & Conversion Factory |
| transaction-level | Per-obligation funding gate: sum(disbursements) <= sum(obligations) per document | Funding-gate section of the reconciliation report; reject ledger | Every batch (daily) | Finance (review) + Factory (produce) |

**5.3 Troubleshooting:**
| symptom | probable_cause | steps_to_resolve | escalation_contact |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Batch exits non-zero / not load-ready | Control-total mismatch or funding-gate trip | Read reconciliation report; triage rejects by typed reason; correct at source. | TBD-SME |

## 6. Interface Requirements
**6.1 Message exchanges:**
| id | name | direction | format | trigger | acknowledgement | success_criteria |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| MX-1 | Obligation/disbursement daily file | outbound | pipe-delimited (MOMENTUM-OBLIGATION-IMPORT) | FMS nightly batch completion | ESB delivery ack + Momentum import disposition | Batch load-ready: rows reconcile, $ totals match, every journal/funding gate passes |

**6.2 File mapping** (17 fields, generated from the contract dataclass):
| position | system_field_name | ifams_field_name | description | ifams_table_field | max_length | format |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | FMS.OBLIGATION_ID | obligation_id | Legacy FMS obligation document number (natural key) | OBL_IMPORT.OBLIGATION_ID | 14 | CHAR |
| 2 | FMS.FISCAL_YEAR | fiscal_year | Posting fiscal year | OBL_IMPORT.FISCAL_YEAR | 4 | NUM CCYY |
| 3 | FMS.ACCOUNTING_PERIOD | accounting_period | Accounting period within fiscal year | OBL_IMPORT.ACCOUNTING_PERIOD | 2 | NUM 1-12 |
| 4 | FMS.LINE_NUMBER | line_number | Line number within the obligation document | OBL_IMPORT.LINE_NUMBER | 5 | NUM |
| 5 | FMS.TXN_TYPE | txn_type | Canonical transaction type (OBLIGATION \| DISBURSEMENT) | OBL_IMPORT.TXN_TYPE | 12 | ENUM |
| 6 | FMS.VENDOR_ID | vendor_id | Vendor identifier (synthetic; production = SAM UEI) | OBL_IMPORT.VENDOR_ID | 12 | CHAR |
| 7 | FMS.TAFS | tafs | Treasury Appropriation Fund Symbol | OBL_IMPORT.TAFS | 21 | CHAR |
| 8 | FMS.APPROPRIATION | appropriation | Momentum fund from the legacy fund crosswalk | OBL_IMPORT.APPROPRIATION | 12 | CHAR |
| 9 | FMS.OBJECT_CLASS | object_class | OMB object class of the spending | OBL_IMPORT.OBJECT_CLASS | 3 | NUM |
| 10 | FMS.USSGL_ACCOUNT | ussgl_account | USSGL account (validated against fiscal-year chart) | OBL_IMPORT.USSGL_ACCOUNT | 6 | NUM |
| 11 | FMS.OBLIGATION_AMOUNT | obligation_amount | Obligation amount (Decimal, 2dp) | OBL_IMPORT.OBLIGATION_AMOUNT | 18 | DEC 2dp |
| 12 | FMS.DISBURSEMENT_AMOUNT | disbursement_amount | Disbursement amount (Decimal, 2dp) | OBL_IMPORT.DISBURSEMENT_AMOUNT | 18 | DEC 2dp |
| 13 | FMS.POP_START_DATE | pop_start_date | Period-of-performance start | OBL_IMPORT.POP_START_DATE | 10 | ISO YYYY-MM-DD |
| 14 | FMS.POP_END_DATE | pop_end_date | Period-of-performance end | OBL_IMPORT.POP_END_DATE | 10 | ISO YYYY-MM-DD |
| 15 | FMS.TXN_DATE | txn_date | Transaction date (converted from legacy CCYYDDD) | OBL_IMPORT.TXN_DATE | 10 | ISO YYYY-MM-DD |
| 16 | FMS.DESCRIPTION | description | Free-text line description (pipe-stripped on emit) | OBL_IMPORT.DESCRIPTION | 60 | CHAR |
| 17 | FMS.SOURCE_SYSTEM | source_system | Originating legacy system tag | OBL_IMPORT.SOURCE_SYSTEM | 8 | CHAR |

**6.3 Web-service mapping.** Not applicable — this interface is file-based. §6.3 (web-service mapping) is retained in the template for SOAP/REST interfaces in the portfolio; no WSDL artifacts have been provided by the customer yet.

## 7. Interface Verification
| method | success_criteria |
| --- | --- |
| Unit | Slice test suite green (parser, every reject reason, funding gate, round-trip). |
| Functional | Clean fixture converts load-ready (exit 0); seeded-defect fixture trips the gate (exit 1). |
| Load | Cutover-volume batch within window (TBD-CUSTOMER volumes). |
| SIT | End-to-end through ESB to Momentum test environment with acks. |
| IST | Joint execution with CGI SI against Momentum import. |
| End User Validation | Finance SME sign-off on reconciliation evidence. |

## 8. Interface Controls
| control_id | control_activity | control_technique | implementation_detail |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| IN-1.1 | Completeness of records transferred | Row accounting invariant | reconciliation.py asserts lines_in == accepted + rejected on every batch; CI gate fails otherwise. |
| IN-1.2 | Accuracy of financial amounts | Dollar control totals | Legacy-side and emitted-side obligation/disbursement totals compared to the cent (Decimal, never float). |
| IN-2.1 | Funds control integrity | Per-obligation funding gate | sum(disbursements) <= sum(obligations) enforced per document pre-emit and re-asserted by the import simulator post-emit. |
| IN-2.2 | Error visibility and resubmission | Typed reject ledger | Every rejected line carries a machine-readable reason code from the shared taxonomy; no silent drops. |

## 9. Implementation & Cutover
**9.1 Conversion considerations.** Legacy fund codes adopt the ACS crosswalk (stand-in FUND_CROSSWALK with 4 entries pending the authoritative crosswalk, Q-REF-2); USSGL validated against a 8-account stand-in chart (Q-REF-1); legacy CCYYDDD dates normalized to ISO. Converted-data interactions: Yes — converted historical obligations must reconcile with daily interface postings so open-document balances remain correct across cutover.

**9.2 Transition approach:**
- Run factory conversion in shadow mode against daily extracts; compare recon evidence to FMS control reports.
- Wave cutover: switch Momentum import to factory-emitted files; FMS posting disabled for the document family.
- Hypercare: daily reconciliation review with Finance for the first close cycle.

**Rollback.** Factory emit is non-destructive: rollback = revert Momentum import to prior source and replay FMS posting; emitted batches are versioned and idempotent so no partial state persists.

## 10. Test Scripts
| id | name | description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| TS-1 | Clean batch load rehearsal | convert.py on the clean synthetic fixture; expect exit 0, load_ready true. |
| TS-2 | Funding-gate trip | Fixture with disbursement > obligation; expect exit 1 and FUNDS_GATE finding. |
| TS-3 | Reject taxonomy coverage | Each typed reject reason exercised by at least one test record. |
